scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE ___ ADDL. CITY CIVIL AND SESSIONS JUDGE
AT BENGALURU (CCH-__)

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT (HAND LOAN) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued. The Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru. Hence, this Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. This Hon’ble Court also has pecuniary jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit.

LIMITATION

2. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation. The hand loan was advanced on 15.03.2024, and the suit is being filed within three years thereof. Accordingly, the suit is within limitation under the Limitation Act, 1963.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

3. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) to the Defendant as a hand loan. The amount was transferred via NEFT from the Plaintiff’s bank account to the Defendant’s bank account, bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456, as evidenced by Annexure P-1.

4. There was no written agreement between the parties. The Defendant acknowledged receipt of the said loan amount through WhatsApp messages, copies of which are annexed as Annexure P-2.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

5. The Plaintiff demanded repayment of the hand loan amount. The Defendant failed to repay the said amount by 20.06.2024. The Plaintiff treats 20.06.2024 as the date of default.

6. The Plaintiff thereafter made repeated oral demands for repayment. The Plaintiff also caused issuance of a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding amount. A copy of the legal notice is annexed as Annexure P-3. Despite the said demands and notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the amount.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

7. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only).

8. The Plaintiff claims simple interest at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 (date of default) until realization, as more particularly set out below.

LEGAL BASIS

9. The Defendant is bound to repay the hand loan received from the Plaintiff. The Defendant’s failure to repay constitutes breach of the obligation undertaken, and the Plaintiff is entitled to recovery of the outstanding amount.

10. Without prejudice, the Plaintiff relies upon Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 to the extent applicable, and seeks interest and costs as permissible in law. Pendente lite and future interest are sought under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

11. The cause of action arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount upon demand, and continues thereafter on each day the Defendant remains in default, including after issuance of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

12. For the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}} (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_IN_WORDS}}), comprising:
    (a) Principal: Rs. 15,00,000; and
    (b) Pre-suit interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 to the date of institution i.e., {{DATE_OF_INSTITUTION}}, amounting to Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}.

13. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on the plaint under the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

INTEREST

14. The Plaintiff claims interest as follows:

    (a) Pre-suit interest at 12% per annum on Rs. 15,00,000 from 20.06.2024 (date of default) till the date of institution of the suit i.e., {{DATE_OF_INSTITUTION}};

    (b) Pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit, from the date of institution of the suit till the date of decree; and

    (c) Future (post-decree) interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit, from the date of decree till realization.

PRAYER

15. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

(a) Pass a judgment and decree directing the Defendant to pay the Plaintiff the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only);

(b) Award interest at 12% per annum on the principal sum from 20.06.2024 (date of default) till the date of institution of the suit;

(c) Award pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of
